issue_title: Validate swagger specs against the swagger spec json schema
issue_body: The Swagger spec has a [json schema](https://github.com/wordnik/swagger-spec/tree/master/schemas).  We should validate downloaded specs against this schema to make it easier to detect any errors.

issue_title: Don't raise new exception
issue_body:
https://github.com/django-extensions/django-extensions/blob/master/django_extensions/management/email_notifications.py#L79

the raise e part is causing it to lose the exception information. It should be changed to just raise.

```
    def execute(self, *args, **options):
        """Overriden in order to send emails on unhandled exception.

        If an unhandled exception in ``def handle(self, *args, **options)``
        occurs and `--email-exception` is set or `self.email_exception` is
        set to True send an email to ADMINS with the traceback and then
        reraise the exception.

        """
        try:
            super(EmailNotificationCommand, self).execute(*args, **options)
        except Exception as e:
            if (options.get('email_exception', False) or getattr(self, 'email_exception', False)):
                self.send_email_notification(include_traceback=True)
            raise e
```

to 

```
    def execute(self, *args, **options):
        """Overriden in order to send emails on unhandled exception.

        If an unhandled exception in ``def handle(self, *args, **options)``
        occurs and `--email-exception` is set or `self.email_exception` is
        set to True send an email to ADMINS with the traceback and then
        reraise the exception.

        """
        try:
            super(EmailNotificationCommand, self).execute(*args, **options)
        except Exception:
            if (options.get('email_exception', False) or getattr(self, 'email_exception', False)):
                self.send_email_notification(include_traceback=True)
            raise
```

http://www.ianbicking.org/blog/2007/09/re-raising-exceptions.html
https://docs.python.org/2/tutorial/errors.html

By raising 'e' you're causing it to lose all that valuable traceback information and all you get is an error occured line 79 of email_notification.py

By raising without 'e' you get the full traceback.

issue_title: Edge requests missing callback arguments for batch
issue_body:
Edge requests such as `AdAccount.get_campaigns` do not have `success` and `failure` arguments so the they could be passed on to `request.add_to_batch` from `FacebookRequest` which in turn accepts those arguments.

As a result, if you are trying, as an example, to get the campaigns from a list of accounts in batched requests, it's not possible to set callbacks.

Figure this is generated, therefore no PR.

issue_title: Linkify creates http link in mailto link when host contains a hyphen
issue_body: Linkify creates http links **in** mailto links if the mail host name contains a hyphen `-` (and `parse_email=True`). The http link starts with the hyphen, even if host names mustn't start with hyphens at all (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hostname#Restrictions_on_valid_hostnames). ```py >>> linkify('foo@exa-mple.com', parse_email=True) 'foo@exa-mple.com' ```

The training coordinator should respond in a meaningful way if he is not ready to serve.

issue_title: Accessing external app in websocket handler with Pulsar 1.2 throws TypeError
issue_body:
With Pulsar 1.1.3. we can access an external app using `yield from pulsar.get_application('app')` from a websocket handler. However, upon updating to pulsar 1.2, this same code throws ``TypeError: can't pickle generator objects``. Is this the expected behavior? If so, what is the correct design pattern to access an external app using a websocket handler?

Here is a simplified example of our websocket handler:

```python
import asyncio
import pulsar
from pulsar.apps import ws, wsgi


class Echo(ws.WS):

    def on_bytes(self, websocket, msg):
        websocket._loop.call_soon(
            pulsar.ensure_future, self._call(websocket, msg))

    @asyncio.coroutine
    def _call(self, websocket, msg):
        # This is actually an independent app, not wsgi
        app = yield from pulsar.get_application('wsgi')
        # Do some things with app
        websocket.write(msg)
        websocket.write_close()


class Site(wsgi.LazyWsgi):

    def setup(self, environ):
        return wsgi.WsgiHandler(
            [ws.WebSocket('/', Echo())])

def server(**kwargs):
    return wsgi.WSGIServer(callable=Site(), **kwargs)
```

The client uses aiohttp, like this:

```python 
import asyncio
import aiohttp

@asyncio.coroutine
def main():
    client = yield from aiohttp.ws_connect('http://127.0.0.1:8060/')
    client.send_bytes(b"hello")
    try:
        while True:
            resp = yield from client.receive()
            if resp.tp == aiohttp.MsgType.close:
                break
            print(resp.data)
    finally:
        yield from client.close()
```

issue_title: Cannot manually expire a coupon redemption on an account
issue_body:
[Documentation](https://dev.recurly.com/docs/remove-a-coupon-from-an-account) suggests that it is possible to expire single coupon redemption at a time. However, this raises a `NotFoundError`, with the following stack trace:
```python
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/recurly/resource.py", line 673, in delete
    self.raise_http_error(response)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/recurly/resource.py", line 685, in raise_http_error
    raise exc_class(response_xml)
NotFoundError: No route matches [DELETE] "/v2/accounts/{{replaced account code}}/redemptions/{{replaced redemption uuid}}s/{{replaced redemption uuid}}"
```

Correct url for expiring the coupon redemption seems to be
```
https://:subdomain.recurly.com/v2/accounts/:account_code/redemptions/:uuid
```
This is according to above mentioned documentation and by trying it out by monkey patching the `delete_url` method on `Redemption` class.

Current implementation of `delete_url()` is as follows:
```python
    def delete_url(self):
      return self._url + "s/" + self.uuid
```

But the `redemption._url` (returned by api) is already in the form
```
https://your-subdomain.recurly.com/v2/accounts/:account_id/redemptions/:redemption_uuid
```
and thus concatenating the `"s/"` and `uuid` once more breaks it.

issue_title: Avoid dependency on pyOpenSSL when using Python 2.7.9
issue_body:
Many enhancements of the Python 3 ssl module have been backported to Python 2.7.9.
In particular, the class `SSLContext()` is available and could be used instead of relying on the pyOpenSSL module. This would avoid the inclusion of a lot of new dependencies: pyopenssl, cryptography, pyasn1, enum34, pycparser, cffi.

I suggest to change [riak/transports/security.py](https://github.com/basho/riak-python-client/blob/2.2.0/riak/transports/security.py) not to use `six.PY2` to decide whether to use the pyOpenSSL, and instead check for the actual availability of `ssl.SSLContext`.

issue_title: Python client adds unexpected `riak@` to certificate CN check [JIRA: CLIENTS-753]
issue_body:
In https://github.com/basho/riak-python-client/blame/master/riak/transports/pbc/connection.py#L133 the prefix `riak@` is added to the SSL certificate host check. Therefore the CN or SAN records of the server certificate need to be `riak@<hostname>` or riak@<ip_addr>`, whereas it's common practice to issue server certificate for the host-name or ip address of the server. The client behaviour is therefore unexpected. Furthermore, this behaviour is unique to the python and ruby clients and generally completely undocumented in Riak's server documentation.

The test certificate has a CN record of `riak@127.0.0.1`, which should be changed accordingly.

issue_title: Scheduler only hosts on unix socket when run in the background
issue_body:
Support for hosting the central scheduler on a unix socket was added, which is nice, but the scheduler ignores the `--unix-socket` argument from the command line when `--background` is not also supplied.  

This will work properly, and the scheduler will listen on the provided unix socket:
```
luigid --unix-socket /path/to/socket --background
```

With this command, the scheduler will still listen on the default port (8082):
```
luigid --unix-socket /path/to/socket
```

Fixing this would be a simple matter of passing the `unix_socket` argument onto the call to `server.run` in the case where the server is not daemonized, but was there a reason this functionality was left out in the first place?  If so, it probably ought to be in the documentation; as is, reading it gives me the impression that I should be able to tell the scheduler to listen on a unix socket regardless of whether it's running in the background.

issue_title: Invalid instance caching on TaskParameter
issue_body:
<!---
We use GitHub issues mainly for tracking bugs and feature requests.
Questions for how to use luigi can be sent to the mailing list.

Currently, there are no strict procedures or guidelines for submitting issues.
In short, please just use common sense.

Common sense includes this at bare-minimum:

 * search for similar issues posted before creating a new issue.
 * Use markdown to format all code/logs. Issues which are hard to read
   when rendered on GitHub might be closed with a friendly reminder of this.
 * If applicable, reading relevant parts of the documentation.

Also, add steps to reproduce the bug, if applicable. Sample code would be nice too :)

For more information on how to submit valuable contributions,
see https://opensource.guide/how-to-contribute/#how-to-submit-a-contribution
-->


I use task parameters to dynamically build trees at runtime:


```python

class SomeTask(Task):

    query = Parameter()
    configuration = Parameter()

    def requires(self):
        return {
            "query": MoveToHdfsTask(
                upstream_task=EnsureExistsOnLocalFilesystemTask(
                    path=self.query)),
            "configuration": MoveToHdfsTask(
                upstream_task=EnsureExistsOnLocalFilesystemTask(
                    path=self.configuration))
        }
```

However `hash(self.input()["query"])` and `hash(self.input()["configuration"])` are the same, despite them having different parameters (albeit, 2 levels deep). Because of this, the instance cacher is kicking in and (incorrectly) causing only one of the two tasks to run.

Is there a canonical way to build up task trees like this? Am I doing it correctly? Should I add a "salt" parameter to the top-level task to invalidate the cache? Should they have the same hash in this instance?

issue_title: boto3.get_products() returns PriceList is List of Strings (Expected: List of Dicts)
issue_body:
**Issue:** when issuing a call to get_products() the return of "PriceList" is a list of strings.
**Expected Behavior:** when issuing a call to get_products() the return of "PriceList" is a list of dicts.

**Steps to Reproduce:** run the following:

```#!/usr/bin/env python

import boto3

pricing_client = boto3.client('pricing', region_name='us-east-1')

service_code = 'AmazonEC2'
product_filters = [
    {'Type': 'TERM_MATCH',
     'Field': 'volumeType',
     'Value': 'General Purpose'}
]

products_response = pricing_client.get_products(ServiceCode=service_code, Filters=product_filters)

print("Printing Products...")
print(products_response)
print("Printing First Product")
print(products_response['PriceList'][0])
print(products_response['PriceList'][0].__class__)
# returns <type 'unicode'>
# expected <<type 'dict'>
print(products_response['PriceList'][0]['product'])
# print(products_response['PriceList'][0]['product']) fails with 
# Traceback (most recent call last):
#   File "./pricing_returning_unicode.py", line 21, in <module>
#     print(products_response['PriceList'][0]['product'])
# TypeError: string indices must be integers
```

**Impact:**
Users of get_products() are not able to do something like: `print(products_response['PriceList'][0]['product']` - this will return an error `TypeError: string indices must be integers`

**Environment:**
- OS: macOS 10.13.4
- python version: either Python 2.7.10 or Python 3.6.5
- boto3 version: boto3 1.7.23

issue_title: UnseekableStreamError constructor triggers UnicodeDecodeError
issue_body:
Hi boto team,

 I have experienced the following event while attempting to send a request using a 'bytearray' payload representing an image:
* A network event triggers request.reset_stream() - https://github.com/boto/botocore/blob/develop/botocore/endpoint.py#L183
* My input type is 'bytearray', and thus triggers an UnseekableStreamError
* The constructor of the UnseekableStreamError attempts to call string.format(), however since my payload is not a valid 'ascii' encoded string, this triggers a UnicodeDecodeException.

This issue is related to https://github.com/boto/botocore/issues/1359, in that the problem would be avoided if bytearray types are omitted from the seek(). 

I'm not a python expert, but I think the solution is simply to convert the following line https://github.com/boto/botocore/blob/develop/botocore/exceptions.py#L302:

from

fmt = ('Need to rewind the stream {stream_object}, but stream '
           'is not seekable.')

to

fmt = ('Need to rewind the stream {stream_object!a}, but stream '
           'is not seekable.')

issue_title: Wrong mode mask when adding files to the index
issue_body:
0fdf6c3aaff49494c47aaeb0caa04b3016e10a26 changed the mode mask from `0o111` to `0o100`. We're encountering issues when committing files marked as executable:

After committing such a file with GitPython, our repo shows the following behavior:
```
# git status
On branch develop
nothing to commit, working directory clean
```
`git status` shows everything as clean (as it should be).

But if we `git stash` the changes:
```
# git stash
error: feeding unmodified <file> to diffcore
error: feeding unmodified <file> to diffcore
Saved working directory and index state WIP on develop: 6cdd426 ... (10136)
HEAD is now at 6cdd426 ... (10136)
```

And the stash turns up empty:
```
# git stash show
```

This is an issue for us because we end up in a dirty repo and can't execute some git commands anymore without resetting/stashing some inexistent changes.

I don't know what the rational behind the `0o100` mask is, but `0o111` works for our use case.

issue_title: Renaming objects causes error
issue_body:
Creating a Block in a new NIX file and then attempting to change its name causes an error:

```python
import nixio

nixfile = nixio.File.open("/tmp/foo.hdf5", nixio.FileMode.Overwrite)
block = nixfile.create_block("blockname1", "blocktype")
block.name = "anothername"
```
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "foo.py", line 4, in <module>
    block.name = "anothername"
AttributeError: can't set attribute
```

According to the [API Documentation](https://g-node.github.io/nixpy/api_data.html#block), this should work.

The same occurs with other object types (tested Groups and DataArrays).

issue_title: Mapbox fails to render the points if the input DataFrame start row index is not zero
issue_body:
Hi,

Mapbox does not work if the DataFrame index does is not zero. It produces an incorrect json output with extra comma at the very first of the json file. An easy fix for it right now is to reset_index of the input DataFrame. The source of issue is at:

issue_title: Display pip traceback if errors during install
issue_body:
If you try to run the installer on a fresh Debian LXC you'll get the following error:

```
Failure occurred. Do you want to cleanup by removing /root/blah? [Y/N] 
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/root/env/lib/python3.4/site-packages/djangocms_installer/main.py", line 35, in execute
    verbose=config_data.verbose
  File "/root/env/lib/python3.4/site-packages/djangocms_installer/install/__init__.py", line 94, in requirements
    output = subprocess.check_output(['pip'] + args)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.4/subprocess.py", line 620, in check_output
    raise CalledProcessError(retcode, process.args, output=output)
subprocess.CalledProcessError: Command '['pip', 'install', '-q', 'django-cms<3.3', 'django-treebeard>=2.0', 'https://github.com/divio/djangocms-column/archive/master.zip?1451649699.4322', 'https://github.com/divio/djangocms-googlemap/archive/master.zip?1451649699.4322', 'https://github.com/divio/djangocms-inherit/archive/master.zip?1451649699.4322', 'https://github.com/divio/djangocms-link/archive/master.zip?1451649699.4322', 'https://github.com/divio/djangocms-style/archive/master.zip?1451649699.4322', 'easy_thumbnails', 'django-filer>=0.9.12', 'cmsplugin-filer>=0.10.2', 'djangocms-admin-style>=1.0.6', 'djangocms-text-ckeditor>=2.8.0', 'Django<1.9', 'pytz', 'django-reversion>=1.8.7,<1.9', 'django-classy-tags>=0.3.4.1', 'html5lib', 'Pillow>=2.3', 'django-sekizai>=0.7', 'six']' returned non-zero exit status 1

During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/root/env/bin/djangocms", line 11, in <module>
    sys.exit(execute())
  File "/root/env/lib/python3.4/site-packages/djangocms_installer/main.py", line 62, in execute
    install.cleanup_directory(config_data)
  File "/root/env/lib/python3.4/site-packages/djangocms_installer/install/__init__.py", line 125, in cleanup_directory
    if strtobool(choice) or config_data.noinput:
  File "/usr/lib/python3.4/distutils/util.py", line 317, in strtobool
    raise ValueError("invalid truth value %r" % (val,))
ValueError: invalid truth value ''
```
There are 2 things here:
* There's no default choice to the last question, causing the installer to raise an exception
* The `pip` failure comes from the fact that there were some libs missing that prevented the installation of Pillow (`libjpeg-dev zlib1g-dev python3-dev gcc`) and these are correctly reported when trying to run `pip install Pillow` but are hidden in the error reported by the installer. It would be really great if the installer could give the full pip traceback

issue_title: resolver dependency contamination
issue_body:
Hello

I see a weird behavior on the version resolution on pipenv update, for dependencies where the latest version should be avoided.

For example, pycodestyle latest version is 2.4.0. Some dependencies such as flake8 3.5.0 adds the restriction "<2.4.0". But the resolver fails at the end with the message

> Could not find a version that matches pyflakes<1.7.0,==2.0.0,>=1.5.0

While reasonably the "==2.0.0" should have been skipped since no one ever restrict to this version. We can see this behavior in the logs:
```
$ pipenv update --clear --verbose
...
pycodestyle (from -r /tmp/pipenv-bvm1pyac-requirements/pipenv-jtt7r2p0-constraints.txt (line 4))
...

Finding the best candidates
...
  found candidate pycodestyle==2.4.0 (constraint was <any>)
...

Finding secondary dependencies:
..
  flake8-comprehensions==1.4.1 requires flake8!=3.2.0, flake8-comprehensions==1.4.1, mccabe<0.7.0,>=0.6.0, pycodestyle<2.4.0,>=2.0.0, pyflakes<1.7.0,>=1.5.0
...
pycodestyle==2.4.0        requires pycodestyle==2.4.0
...
  flake8==3.5.0             requires flake8==3.5.0, mccabe<0.7.0,>=0.6.0, pycodestyle<2.4.0,>=2.0.0, pyflakes<1.7.0,>=1.5.0
...

New dependencies found in this round:
...
  adding ['pycodestyle', '<2.4.0,==2.4.0,>=2.0.0,>=2.3', '[]']
```
From now on, the resolution couldn't succeed since "<2.4.0" and =="2.4.0" are obviously incompatible.

Workaround: recopy the restriction "<2.4.0" in my Pipfile.

Happens with pycodestyle and pyflakes (restricted to "<1.7.0" by flake8 3.5.0)

issue_title: create requirements.txt without virtualenv?
issue_body:
hi! Is there a way to create a `requrirements.txt` file without creating a virtualenv? I'm trying to build a series of lambdas that each have separate `Pipfile`s.

After cding into each lambdas fodler, I'm running `pipenv lock -r | pip install -r /dev/stdin --target src`. Unfortunately, it creates a virtualenv per invocation which wastes time.

Is there an easy way to just translate the Pipfile.lock -> requirements.txt format without creating the virtualenv?

Thanks!

issue_title: Feature Request: Add custom groups
issue_body:
I see that today the different install groups default and develop are hardcoded. Is there a plan to have custom groups? For example, the very common pipeline dev, stage, production could be handled nicely:

```
[packages]
# default packages here
[dev-packages]
# dev packages here
[stage-packages]
# stage packages here
[production-packages]
# production packages here
[somethingelse-packages]
# something else packages here
```

issue_title: Replacement of interlinking requirements.txt files
issue_body:
Hi there,

I'd love to be able to replace our use of requirements.txt files with Pipenv. However, I don't quite see how yet, as our use of the requirements.txt files seems to be rather unique. Our setup consists of a top-level application project ('mainproject'), our own framework 'Pillar', and a couple of Pillar extensions. To manage the requirements, each of those have their own requirements.txt file that refers to the requirements.txt files of their dependencies. This allowed us to pin versions for each project and still have per-project files.

- `pillar/requirements.txt` contains all the framework's requirements, all with pinned versions.
- `extension1/requirements.txt` starts with `-r ../pillar/requirements.txt` to link in the framework requirements, then continues to list the extension-speciifc ones.
- `mainproject/requirements.txt`: starts with:
  ```
  -r ../extension1/requirements.txt
  -r ../extension2/requirements.txt
  -r ../pillar/requirements.txt
  ```
  and then lists its own requirements, if any.

This setup allows us to quickly enable/disable certain extensions, and avoid repeating requirements and pinned versions. We want to get rid of those files, though, as pinning each version is a nightmare to maintain (as you well know).

How would such a setup translate to Pipenv? We don't push Pillar to PyPi, nor do we push the extensions. In the end it's all collected into a docker container for deployment simply by checking out certain Git branches; there is no `python3 setup.py bdist` invocations to create distribution packages.

I've tried adding something like this to `extension1/Pipfile`:

```
[packages]
pillar = {source = '../pillar', editable = true, version = "*"}
```

This doesn't work,  though, as `pipenv install` in the main project gives us:

```
Virtualenv location: /home/sybren/.virtualenvs/mainproject-O7T8y2wV
Pipfile.lock not found, creating...
Locking [dev-packages] dependencies...
Locking [packages] dependencies...

Warning: Your dependencies could not be resolved. You likely have a mismatch in your sub-dependencies.
  You can use $ pipenv install --skip-lock to bypass this mechanism, then run $ pipenv graph to inspect the situation.
  Hint: try $ pipenv lock --pre if it is a pre-release dependency.
Could not find a version that matches pillar (from -r /tmp/pipenv-jmw6x1mc-requirements/pipenv-x6d8in2v-constraints.txt (line 2))
No versions found
Was https://pypi.org/simple reachable?
```

For me it would be ideal if we could specify "include section X from pipfile Y", something along those lines in `mainproject/Pipfile`:

```
[packages]
__include__ = {pipfile = "../pillar/Pipfile"}
otherdependency = "*"
```

issue_body:
I've been thinking about the usage of `Pipfile`, and this question kept bugging me:
Is the `Pipfile` for a project meant to be the same file used both for the dev and prod environments (and any other environment, like QA setups and such, if it applies)?

If yes, how do you do so? 
In development, users will usually install one (or many) packages in editable mode, which doesn't match how it should be installed when in production.

issue_title: __PYVENV_LAUNCHER__ overriding specified path to exectuable
issue_body:
see here: https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/blob/ac325f34b73c534fd19204400afa598bf63ea301/setuptools/command/easy_install.py#L1938

Running inside a virtualenv (created with pyvenv) created the env variable `__PYVENV_LAUNCHER__`. When I want to run an executable outside the virtualenv, the only way I can do so is if I unset `__PYVENV_LAUNCHER__`, since having this env var present overrides the path to executable specified.

issue_title: Disabling tracebacks in response should be much easier
issue_body:
By default Klein displays tracebacks in response if 500 error occurs. This is ok and useful for debugging but I think there should be some optional argument for run() disabling tracebacks and there should be information in documentation about this. 

Unless I'm missing something figuring out how to disable tracebacks in production is currently not obvious and requires you to go into Klein code find out it uses Site() object under the hood, find out this object has displayTraceback attribute and set this attribute to False on class level. 

So something like this:

```python
from klein import Klein
from twisted.web.server import Site

app = Klein()
Site.displayTracebacks = False

@app.route('/')
def home(request):
    raise
    return 'Hello, world!'

app.run("localhost", 8080)
```

I think that thing like this should not require that much thinking, it would be better to simply have optional debug=True/False argument for `Klein().run()` that would set this for you.

issue_title: GridFS.get messing when file is not found
issue_body:
`GridFS.get` uses `find_one` to retrieve the requested file from the collection.
However nothing is done if the returned value is None, which leads to...

```python
>>> gridout = yield GridFS.get('dummy')
>>> gridout
None
>>> not gridout
False
>>> bool(gridout)
True
>>> type(gridout)
<class 'txmongo._gridfs.grid_file.GridOut'>
```
:trollface:

Of course the returned GridOut object is not usable

issue_title: Streamparse calling next_tuple continuously
issue_body:
Everything is working in my quite basic topology except the simple Kafka spout that I wrote - it seems to just continuously call "next_tuple". My bolts are fairly slow, so the system seems to very quickly explode in memory as the spout adds too many messages. If the problem/solution is non-obvious from the information provided here, I can try to modify one of the example projects to provide a working repro.

Launching the topology, I tried to set topology.max.spout.pending to 1 to prevent it from adding too many messages to the topology.

    lein run -m streamparse.commands.run/-main topologies/.clj -t 100 --option 'topology.max.spout.pending=1' --option 'topology.workers=1' --option 'topology.acker.executors=1' 

However, the result is this, despite the bolts being much slower than the spout:

    24790 [Thread-16-metadata-spout] INFO  backtype.storm.spout.ShellSpout - Shell msg: ----NEXT TUPLE----
    24942 [Thread-16-metadata-spout] INFO  backtype.storm.spout.ShellSpout - Shell msg: ----NEXT TUPLE----
    24944 [Thread-16-metadata-spout] INFO  backtype.storm.spout.ShellSpout - Shell msg: ----NEXT TUPLE----
    24946 [Thread-16-metadata-spout] INFO  backtype.storm.spout.ShellSpout - Shell msg: ----NEXT TUPLE----
    25143 [Thread-16-metadata-spout] INFO  backtype.storm.spout.ShellSpout - Shell msg: ----NEXT TUPLE----
    25144 [Thread-16-metadata-spout] INFO  backtype.storm.spout.ShellSpout - Shell msg: ----NEXT TUPLE----
    25350 [Thread-16-metadata-spout] INFO  backtype.storm.spout.ShellSpout - Shell msg: ----NEXT TUPLE----
    ......

My simple Kafka spout:

    class MetadataSpout(Spout):
    
        def initialize(self, stormconf, context):
            self.log('----CONFIG: %s----' % stormconf)
            k = KafkaClient(os.getenv('KAFKA'))
            self.consumer = SimpleConsumer(k, 'vacuum', 'metadata')
    
        def next_tuple(self):
            self.log('----NEXT TUPLE----')
            messages = self.consumer.get_messages(count=os.getenv('BATCH_COUNT', 20))
            self.emit([json.dumps([m.message.value for m in messages])])


My bolts have just the default ack configuration, but take a significant amount of time to complete the process() method. I cannot figure out how they could be the problem, but I can post if they're relevant.

issue_title: Fix interpolation when there are not eight neighbours present
issue_body:
Currently, when doing the interpolation, the ``interpolate_bilinear_lonlat`` function in Cython searches for the four closest neighbours as if it was on a regular grid. However, in some places in the HEALPix grid, a given point is not surrounded by four cells, but three. The following example shows pixel index 653824 in a HEALPix grid with nside=256 in ring order, and its neighbours:

![debug](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/314716/31053864-216a5d98-a69e-11e7-9ace-a57c9296690d.png)

The cross indicates the point where I was trying to interpolate, and where I got NaN with the current function. The position of the cross is lon=0.0 lat=-41.730291566995895

We should figure out what the correct behavior should be in this case - healpy deals with this case as follows:

```python
In [4]: healpy.get_interp_weights(256, 0.0, -41.730291566995895, nest=False, lonlat=True)
Out[4]: 
(array([653824, 653825, 655871, 654848]),
 array([ 0.40037327,  0.        ,  0.29981337,  0.29981337]))
```

I guess this must be doing some kind of [barycentric interpolation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barycentric_coordinate_system#Interpolation_on_a_triangular_unstructured_grid)?

issue_title: what's the difference between sigma and kernel_size in quikshit?
issue_body:
kernel_size : float, optional (default 5)

        Width of Gaussian kernel used in smoothing the sample density. Higher means fewer clusters.

        sigma : float, optional (default 0)

        Width for Gaussian smoothing as preprocessing. Zero means no smoothing.

And i saw `sigma controls the scale of the local density approximation` in [Comparison of segmentation and superpixel algorithms](http://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/auto_examples/plot_segmentations.html).

But i saw `Kernel size. The pixel density and its modes are estimated by using a Parzen window estimator with a Gaussian kernel of the specified size (vl_quickshift_set_kernel_size). The larger the size, the larger the neighborhoods of pixels considered.` in [Quick shift image segmentation](http://www.vlfeat.org/api/quickshift.html)

issue_title: Pypi上aliyun-python-sdk-core-v3的代码和Github代码不一致
issue_body:
目前Pypi上的aliyun-python-sdk-core-v3最新版本是2.8.9，使用时会出现错误：
```
aliyunsdkcore.acs_exception.exceptions.ClientException: SDK.InvalidRegionId Can not find endpoint to access.
```
调查后发现aliyun-python-sdk-core-v3中 client.py 文件中`_resolve_endpoint`和Github上的内容不一样：

Pypi上的版本：
```
    def _resolve_endpoint(self, request):
        return endpoint_resolver.resolve_endpoint(self.__region_id, request, self._location_service)
```

Github上版本：
```
    def _resolve_endpoint(self, request):

        if isinstance(request, CommonRequest) and request.get_domain():
            return request.get_domain()

        endpoint = None
        if request.get_location_service_code() is not None:
            endpoint = self._location_service.find_product_domain(
                self.get_region_id(), request.get_location_service_code(), request.get_product(),
                request.get_location_endpoint_type())
        if endpoint is None:
            endpoint = region_provider.find_product_domain(
                self.get_region_id(), request.get_product())
            if endpoint is None:
                raise ClientException(
                    error_code.SDK_INVALID_REGION_ID,
                    error_msg.get_msg('SDK_INVALID_REGION_ID'))
            if not isinstance(request, AcsRequest):
                raise ClientException(
                    error_code.SDK_INVALID_REQUEST,
                    error_msg.get_msg('SDK_INVALID_REQUEST'))
        return endpoint
```

应该是没有更新Pypi上的代码，希望可以尽快解决。

  File "/opt/python/run/venv/local/lib/python3.4/site-packages/django/db/migrations/loader.py", line 47, in __init__
    self.build_graph()
  File "/opt/python/run/venv/local/lib/python3.4/site-packages/django/db/migrations/loader.py", line 294, in build_graph
    _reraise_missing_dependency(migration, parent, e)
  File "/opt/python/run/venv/local/lib/python3.4/site-packages/django/db/migrations/loader.py", line 264, in _reraise_missing_dependency
    raise exc
  File "/opt/python/run/venv/local/lib/python3.4/site-packages/django/db/migrations/loader.py", line 290, in build_graph
    self.graph.add_dependency(migration, key, parent)
  File "/opt/python/run/venv/local/lib/python3.4/site-packages/django/db/migrations/graph.py", line 116, in add_dependency
    parent
django.db.migrations.graph.NodeNotFoundError: Migration icecream.0001_initial dependencies reference nonexistent parent node ('cities_light', '0004_auto_20151021_1129')
```

In the ./lib/python3.4/site-packages/cities_light/migrations, it indeed has the 0004_auto_20151021_1129.py file.

Why does it still complains the **reference nonexistent parent node**? If I remove all database, migrations, and remigrate, the icecream.0001_initial migrations file won't depend on `('cities_light', '0004_auto_20151021_1129')`, but depend on some timestamp generated today
```
('cities_light', '0004_auto_20151212_1403'),
```

Obviously, this 0004_auto_20151212_1403.py does not exist in the ./lib/python3.4/site-packages/cities_light/migrations. But the django-admin makemigrations and migrate do not complain anything and the Django app is 'working' again.

issue_title: New version of pylint breaks strings checker
issue_body:
Hello.

I used to use this plugin and it gave me correct errors on `'{}'.format('abc')` in Python 2.6. Now that check no longer works with new versions of pylint.

Code:
```
(pylint) sevagh:py-2.6-compat-linting $ cat example.py
#!/usr/bin/env python2

if __name__ == '__main__':
    mystr = 'Bad format string {} {} {}'.format('a',
                                                'b',
                                                'c')

    with open('/tmp/f1.txt', 'r') as a, open('/tmp/f2.txt', 'r') as b:
        print('Two withs in one line!')
(pylint) sevagh:py-2.6-compat-linting $
```

Pylintrc:
```
(pylint) sevagh:py-2.6-compat-linting $ cat .pylintrc
[MASTER]
load-plugins=saltpylint.minpyver,
    saltpylint.strings
minimum-python-version=2.6
[MESSAGES CONTROL]
disable=all
enable=E0598,
    W1320,
    E1320,
    W1321,
    E1231,
    E1322
```

Execution result:
```
(pylint) sevagh:py-2.6-compat-linting $ pylint ./example.py
************* Module example
E:  8, 0: Incompatible Python 2.6 code found: with statement with multiple contexts (minimum-python-version)

------------------------------------------------------------------
Your code has been rated at 0.00/10 (previous run: 0.00/10, +0.00)

(pylint) sevagh:py-2.6-compat-linting $
```

issue_title: release: for security
docs: base template pattern example wrong
issue_body:
The current example is incorrect. It will never set the ``FOO_BASE_TEMPLATE`` to the module's base the template. It should probably be changed to something like this:

```python
# invenio_foo/config.py
FOO_BASE_TEMPLATE = 'invenio_foo/base.html'

# invenio_foo/ext.py
from . import config

class InvenioFoo(object):
    # ...
    def init_config(app):
        # Set FOO_BASE_TEMPLATE to the theme's
        # base template if theme is installed
        if 'BASE_TEMPLATE' in app.config:
            app.config.setdefault(
                'FOO_BASE_TEMPLATE',
                app.config['BASE_TEMPLATE'],
        )
        # If no theme is installed, this will set
        # the base template to the module's
        # base template.
        for k in dir(config):
            if k.startswith('FOO_'):
                app.config.setdefault(
                    k,
                    getattr(config, k))
```

issue_title: global: filter out weird characters in set name
issue_body:
OAI Sets which contain weird characters in their names cannot be rendered in XML outputs, causing issues with the `ListSets` verb (e.g. `u'para\x0bla'`).

A solution would be to integrate some kind of sanitization like the one applied in [`invenio_records_rest.schemas.fields.SanitizedUnicode`](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-records-rest/blob/3d63611e7e85ee5a981ff1faa67d11bc166bdfbe/invenio_records_rest/schemas/fields/sanitizedunicode.py#L21-L44).

issue_title: views: resolving on-the-fly schema references
issue_body:
Currently `invenio-jsonschemas` directly serves the schema from disk, without first resolving `$ref` to other schemas.

E.g.: see https://qa.inspirehep.net/schemas/records/hep.json which contains unresolved `$ref`s.

It would be actually great to resolve those and have a self-contained schema being served each time.

issue_title: Build / publish wheels on Appveyor
issue_body:
Checklist from https://github.com/zopefoundation/zope.proxy/issues/24:

- [x] a copy of `appveyor.yml` from `zope.interface` or similar
- [x] someone with an Appveyor account (e.g. @mgedmin ) to enable this project
- [x] encrypted PyPI upload credentials in the `appveyor.yml` (no change required from the copy in `zope.interface` if @mgedmin is the one who enables the Appveyor account)
- [x] grant **zope.wheelbuilder** PyPI rights
- [x] push the `appveyor.yml` commit to master
- [ ] (optionally) a push of a temporary branch that builds and uploads `AccessControl 4.0b1` wheels

issue_title: Spurious test failure on slow machines
issue_body:
Greetings.  I recently packaged zope.testrunner for the Fedora Linux distribution.  Fedora supports 3 primary architectures: x86_64, i686, and ARM.  We run zope.testrunner's own test suite as part of the build process.  The tests all pass on all 3 architectures, but the ARM builders always report a test failure, because this line is expected:

Total: 321 tests, 0 failures, 0 errors and 0 skipped in N.NNN seconds.

but this is what is actually produced:

Total: 321 tests, 0 failures, 0 errors and 0 skipped in 2 minutes N.NNN seconds.

Is there any way to make the "N minutes" phrase optional on that line?

issue_title: Clarify branch cut for arccos
issue_body:
The doc for `np.arccos` goes into the detail of specifying the branch cut:
```
arccos is a multivalued function: for each x there are infinitely many numbers z such that cos(z) = x.
The convention is to return the angle z whose real part lies in [0, pi].
```
(which, unsurprisingly, matches the C standard).  This is not a complete definition though: it does not specify what is the output for a real input `x` of absolute value greater than 1, in which case both `arccosh(x) * 1j` and `-arccosh(x) * 1j` have a cosine equal to `x`.  From a quick test it seems that the latter is chosen, but it'd be nice if this was specified.

issue_title: QHeaderView.setSectionResizeMode
issue_body: in Qt5 QHeaderView.setResizeMode is deprecated (and thus not existing in pyqt5) and was replaced by QHeaderView.setSectionResizeMode. qtpy should define QHeaderView.setSectionResizeMode if qt4 or pyside is used to not raise an attribute error.

issue_title: Multiple path parameters and multiple endpoints
issue_body:
Hi there,

I currently have a flask_restful Resource with 2 path parameters: one compulsory, and one optional. I followed [this recommendations](http://stackoverflow.com/a/26379214/6487514) of writing it this way:

`api.add_resource(ProductsResource, '/products/<string:slug>', '/products/<string:slug>/<string:color>')`

```
class ProductsResource(Resource):
      @marshal_with(products_fields)
      def get(self, slug, color=None):
           ....
```

Then, i have the following YML documentation in the docstring regarding parameters:

```
...
- name: slug
   in: path
   description: product's slug
   type: string
   required: true
- name: color
   in: path
   description: product's color
   type: string
   required: true
```

When I go to my project on http://localhost:5000/spec, I see that I have 2 endpoint generated:

 * http://localhost:5000/products/{slug}
 * http://localhost:5000/products/{slug}/{color}

But, for both endpoints there are those 2 required parameters, resulting in a swagger error: `Path parameter is defined but is not declared: color`. 

Do you think it would be possible to filter the path parameters based on the endpoints used ? Here it would mean filter out 'color' for the first endpoint.

Thanks,
Pierre.

issue_title: how to use brotli::BrotliCompressor, the correct/intended way?
issue_body:
### Abstract

I appreciate that the brotli library offers a class for stream based compression.
In order to use this class as intended, I have some questions, which the sparse header file didn't answer.

I consider myself as a novice C-Programmer and I'm just following the concept of
[don't make me think](https://www.sensible.com/dmmt.html) - applied to API design ;-)

### General usage

Just from looking at the public method's signatures, I have some expectations.
I would use the BrotliCompression class like this (pseudo-code).

```
c = new BrotliCompressor(params);
partialBuffer = new buffer(c->input_block_size())    // use max partial buffer size for stream
for partialBuffer in hugeInputBuffer {
    c->CopyInputToRingBuffer(partialBuffer)
    c->WriteBrotliData(isLast=false, doFlush=false, size(partialBuffer), &outputBuffer)
}
c->WriteBrotliData(isLast=true, doFlush=true, 0, &outputBuffer)  // final flush
```
I wouldn't expect doing anything more, to write a valid brotli stream, which I can send over wire or save to disk.

##### Questions

* Why are the following methods public, and when to use them?
   * WriteMetaBlock()
   * WriteMetadata()
   * FinishStream
   * BrotliSetCustomDictionary()
   * WriteStreamHeader()
* I didn't use them and the compressor works fine (no errors)
    * IMHO, If they are mandatory, the compressor should [fail fast](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fail-fast)

### Is_Last vs. Do_Flush vs. FinishStream()

I know, when it comes to file IO, what flush() means. But with these three options I'm confused.

##### Questions

* When do I use what?
* What are the consequences?
* What does Is_Last do?
    * What is the consequence of not doing it?
    * Is this mandatory or just a hint?
* When there is a FinishStream(),
    * Why isn't there a StartStream()? (it's all about wording, e.g. have consistent and symmetric naming)

I'm looking forward to a fruitful discussion :-)

Thanks in advance
Martin

issue_title: Avoid grouping by day when calculating quantile mean returns
issue_body:
As alphalens is data frequency agnostic, the [aggregation by day performed when calculating quantile mean returns](https://github.com/quantopian/alphalens/blob/master/alphalens/tears.py#L138 ) doesn't make sense all the time. It could produce better plots for some scenarios, depending on data frequency and data time span, but not for all the possible data given in input to alphalens.

I am suggesting to remove the aggregation by day or to make it optional and in this latter case the aggregation period should be configurable: minute, day, hour, week, whatever.

Also the feature should be applied to all the plots consistently (not only on some of them). Currently it is applied only to plotting.plot_quantile_returns_violin, plotting.plot_cumulative_returns_by_quantile and plotting.plot_mean_quantile_returns_spread_time_series.

issue_title: Django: Custom AUTH_USER_MODEL
issue_body:
Hi,

We're having a bit of trouble with logging `request.user` data after using a custom `AUTH_USER_MODEL` in our Django projects.

Here's the deal as far as I can understand:
- raven's `DjangoClient` checks whether the `request.user` object is a Django user instance [here](https://github.com/getsentry/raven-python/blob/2f6fc1410ee5532698c49c37fe9a721339b097fd/raven/contrib/django/client.py#L66), which in our case isn't.
- Calling [`DjangoClient.get_user_info()`](https://github.com/getsentry/raven-python/blob/2f6fc1410ee5532698c49c37fe9a721339b097fd/raven/contrib/django/client.py#L36) for the same request works fine and provides all the user data we want to log.
- The only blocker is the `isinstance()` check in the first bullet's link above.

I've got two questions here:
- Since [`DjangoClient.get_user_info()`](https://github.com/getsentry/raven-python/blob/2f6fc1410ee5532698c49c37fe9a721339b097fd/raven/contrib/django/client.py#L36) does a lot of `hasattr()` checking, why is the `isinstance()` check necessary? I understand it's a safety check, but I believe it could be skipped as the user data will be extracted safely in `get_user_info()` anyway.
- If that's not possible, how about adding in a check for a custom `AUTH_USER_MODEL` being in place and bypassing the `isinstance()` check only in that case?

I'm sure I might be missing some context here, so let me know what you think is an appropriate way to move forward for projects that use a custom `AUTH_USER_MODEL` and I could work on a pull request.

issue_title: bindings of variables are not carried into FILTERs
issue_body:
My understanding is that variable bindings carry into FILTER constructs.  However, this sometime does not work in rdflib.  I have a short example that shows this, with a FILTER inside a FILTER EXISTS.  My belief is that the binding of ?this from the outermost portion should be visible in the innermost portion, meaning that the ?this = ?valu will only filter out solutions where the two variables have different bindings.  However, all solutions appear to be filtered out, which would happen if the ?this binding(s) from the outermost portion are not making their way into the innermost portion.

This is very similar to the example in SPARQL 1.1 Query Language section 8.3.3.

[bug2-rdflib.txt](https://github.com/RDFLib/rdflib/files/184221/bug2-rdflib.txt)

issue_title: config bug or config documentation confusion
issue_body:
I have run into a few issues recently while generating some simulations for HSC.  One of them is either a config bug or a problem with the config documentation.

The impression I get from the examples/great3/ yaml files and the config wiki is that there are three ways to use a template .yaml file and configure some of the details afterwards.

-  suck in the entire .yaml file as a template, then change some details, for example as in examples/great3/rgc.yaml where it does stuff like
```
template : cgc.yaml
gal.gal_type : real
image.noise.whiten : True
output.dir : real/ground/constant
```
thereby modifying the galaxies from parametric to real, whitening the noise, and changing the output location.

- suck in parts of the .yaml file as a template.  For example as in cgc_psf.yaml
```
psf:
    template : 'cgc.yaml:psf'
```
and as on [the wiki](https://github.com/GalSim-developers/GalSim/wiki/Config-Documentation#template), "The template field may appear anywhere in the config file. Wherever it appears, the contents of the other file will be a starting point for that part of the current config dict, but you can replace or add values by specifying new values for some of the fields."  So this suggests one could later do something like ```psf.index_key : obj_num``` or some such thing, just as in the first case when you suck in the whole file as a template and modify bits later.

- Suck in your template and/or specify things in the .yaml file, but then modify bits when you call the galsim executable at the command line.

The crux of the problem I identified is that the first and third option work, and the second one does not.  It just quietly ignores later modifications if you have sucked in parts of another .yaml file as a template.

Is this the intended behavior?  If so, then the config documentation needs some updating.

Or if it's not the intended behavior, would it be possible to fix the bug and also tag a v1.4.3 so we can used a tagged version for HSC sims?

```python
        # Add body parameters
        for key, value in body_parameters.items():
            if key not in arguments:
                logger.debug("Body parameter '%s' not in function arguments", key)
```

```yaml
  /provenance/:
    post:
      tags:
        - INGEST
      description: creates a new provenance if it does not exist

issue_title: Error validating a body parameter
issue_body:
### Description

Here is the spec.

```yaml
swagger: '2.0'
info:
  title: Connexion issue
  version: '0.0.1'
host: localhost:8080

paths:
  /:
    put:
      operationId: app.put_document
      consumes:
        - text/plain
      parameters:
        - name: body
          in: body
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        200:
          description: OK
```

And here is the app.

```python
import connexion
from connexion import NoContent

def put_document(body):
    return NoContent

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app = connexion.App(__name__, port=8080)
    app.add_api('swagger.yaml')
    app.run()
```

### Expected behaviour

```
$ curl -X PUT --header 'Content-Type: text/plain' -d 'test' 'http://localhost:8080/'
```
returns `200 OK`

### Actual behaviour

Instead it returns `400 BAD REQUEST` with the following body.

```json
{
  "type": "about:blank",
  "title": "Bad Request",
  "status": 400,
  "detail": "None is not of type 'string'"
}
```

### Steps to reproduce

```sh
$ python app.py
$ curl -X PUT --header 'Content-Type: text/plain' -d 'test' 'http://localhost:8080/'
```

### Additional info:

Python 3.5.2
Version: 1.0.112

issue_title: Improve explicit principal compatibility with pykerberos
issue_body:
The authGSSClientInit implementation in WinKerberos attempts to be fully compatible with pykerberos, including support for the "principal" parameter, while also supporting some SSPI specific options (user, domain, and password). The current support for the principal option appears to be incorrect.

First, some background information. On a *nix machine you login locally, then call kinit to get a TGT. Starting with krb5 1.10 you can take advantage of [credential cache collections](http://k5wiki.kerberos.org/wiki/Projects/Credential_cache_collection_improvements) with the addition of kswitch and improvements to kinit to get TGTs for multiple principals. gss_acquire_cred can find the correct credentials in the correct cache for the principal you specify.

On Windows the situation is very different. First, there is no kinit or kswitch, and klist only lists existing tickets or tgts. The documentation for the pszPrincipal parameter of [AcquireCredentialsHandle](https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/aa374713(v=vs.85).aspx) appears to be wrong. How it is supposed to work is a mystery. [Comments here](http://www.kerberos.org/software/samples/gsskrb5/gsskrb5-1011/krb5/krb5cred.c) claim that it is ignored in the Kerberos SSP. [This post](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/microsoft.public.platformsdk.security/Xlo23EMiH9U) from back in 2001 claims it must be NULL.

The solution to this problem appears to be using the principal argument, when provided, as explicit user and domain when calling AcquireCredentialsHandle. For backward compatibility we should continue to support the user and domain parameters, preferring them if provided.

See https://github.com/requests/requests-kerberos/pull/75 for the discussion leading to this ticket.

issue_title: Nonuniform state numbers
issue_body:
When manticore forks, state id's are not assigned in a uniform, incremental way. In the below run of `examples/linux/basic`, we can see that the state ids are 0, 3, and 5, when they should intuitively be 0, 1, and 2. This makes the state id and incorrect source for the actual number of states that were created.

This happens because

1. In the State ctor, `get_new_id` is always called. When state are loaded (unpickled), this ctor executes, incrementing the global state count unnecessarily.
2. In State `__enter__` (copying mechanism), `get_new_id` is explicitly called, in addition to the ctor, which unnecessarily bumps the state count.

There are fixes for these in the dev-events branch, as a result of refactoring this and moving state counting to the executor.

The *usage* section of the [Pypi project page](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/num2words) lists the result of `num2words(42, to='cardinal')` as the ordinal result

### Expected Behaviour

Text should read:

```
>>> num2words(42, to='cardinal')
forty-two
```

or

```
>>> num2words(42, to='ordinal')
forty-second
```


### Actual Behaviour

Text reads

```
>>> num2words(42, to='cardinal')
forty-second
```

issue_title: Ready for production?
issue_body:
We're currently looking into migrating to gke from a managed vserver, where we're running celery on rabbitmq and redis. We don't have very elaborate requirements for delayed task execution: the usual delayed tasks, a few cron jobs and a couple of long running background tasks. 

PSQ looks very interesting as thin layer above pub/sub. My question is: is it considered ready for production? Is anyone using it already?

Also related: What's the best way to use it in CI for testing? In RQ for example, we can declare a queue as synchronous with 

    Queue('low', async=False)

and thus don't need to handle any workers, which seems handy (I haven't used it, though). Or should I just use the gcloud emulator, which *should* work if I'm not mistaken, but brings its fair set of complications to testing, as we'd need to spin up some workers.

Thanks!

issue_body:
See #528 for some history here.

The issue is that requests made using `foo` instead of `s~foo` fail in [strange ways][1].

So as a result we have to use `datastore.helpers._prepare_key_for_request` to strip dataset IDs from keys that get sent to the server. This has accidental caused bugs in the past (e.g. allowing keys from a different dataset `bar` to be inserted into dataset `foo`)

[1]: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-datastore/issues/59

issue_title: PubSub: Sub client not retrieving message again if it doesn't ack
issue_labels: [{'id': 544604244, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p1', 'name': 'priority: p1', 'color': 'ffa03e', 'default': False}, {'id': 77438580, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/type:%20bug', 'name': 'type: bug', 'color': 'd93f0b', 'default': False}, {'id': 77438584, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/type:%20question', 'name': 'type: question', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
I'm experiencing an issue where messages are not retrieved again when a message is not ack'ed. 

In the below code, the self.sub is opened and the main thread sleeps. When the process method fails, the message is not ack'ed and I am expecting it to be resent in the ack deadline of the subscription (10 seconds this case). However, the message is never receieved again in this subclient, unless I completely restart the subclient and open a new callback on the same subscription. The subscription continues to listen to new messages though.

In other threads (such as this one https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/issues/4615) and in the docs, it mentions using `future.result()` or `future.exception()`, but both of these just block and never show a problem has occured.

What is the best way to fix this sort of problem? Additionally, is there a way to get the error to propagate? 

A snippet:
```
import random

from google import auth
from google.auth import exceptions
from google.auth.credentials import AnonymousCredentials
from google.cloud import pubsub


class TestProcessor:

    def process_message(self, message):
        print('processing...')
        if random.randint(0, 1): # Some unforeseen error
            raise ValueError
        print(message)
        message.ack()


class GoogleSubClient:

    def __init__(self, project_id, topic, subscription, message_processor, max_messages=10):
        try:
            credentials, _ = auth.default()
        except exceptions.DefaultCredentialsError:  # For running on emulator
            credentials = AnonymousCredentials()
        self._subscriber = pubsub.SubscriberClient(credentials=credentials)
        self.sub = None
        self.future = None
        self._flow = pubsub.types.FlowControl(max_messages=max_messages)
        self._topic = self._subscriber.topic_path(project=project_id, topic=topic)
        self._subscription = self._subscriber.subscription_path(project=project_id, subscription=subscription)
        self._message_processor = message_processor

    def run(self):
        self.sub = self._subscriber.subscribe(subscription=self._subscription, flow_control=self._flow)
        self.future = self.sub.open(self._message_processor.process_message)
```

pubsub version:
```
Name: google-cloud-pubsub
Version: 0.32.0
```

issue_body:
### tl;dr

The GAPIC-generated class constructors listed below scribble on the fallback `google.api_core.gapic_v1.client_info.DEFAULT_CLIENT_INFO` instance, rather than creating their own copy.

@crwilcox Can you please link in the related codegen issue?

### Analysis

The pattern is that these client classes take an optional `client_info`
parameter:  if not passed, they use the `DEFAULT_CLIENT_INFO` instance.  
In either case, they then assign over its `gapic_version` attribute:


```python

        if client_info is None:
            client_info = (
                google.api_core.gapic_v1.client_info.DEFAULT_CLIENT_INFO)
        client_info.gapic_version = _GAPIC_LIBRARY_VERSION
        self._client_info = client_info
```


I am **certain** that it is inappropriate to scribble on the global
`DEFAULT_CLIENT_INFO` instance.  Rather they should be creating a new
`google.api_core.gapic.client_info.ClientInfo` instance.  I
**believe** that if the user passes in a non-None `client_info`, these
clients should not scribble on it.  E.g.:


```python

        if client_info is None:
            client_info = google.api_core.gapic_v1.client_info.ClientInfo(
                gapic_version = _GAPIC_LIBRARY_VERSION,
            )
        self._client_info = client_info
```

### Offending Classes

- `google.cloud.automl_v1beta1.gapic.auto_ml_client.AutoMlClient`
- `google.cloud.automl_v1beta1.gapic.prediction_service_client.PredictionServiceClient`

issue_title: datalab.storage.Item - allow to upload file from local disk
issue_body:
according to: http://googledatalab.github.io/pydatalab/datalab.storage.html#
The supported function of `datalab.storage.Item` that allows upload is: `write_to`
this function upload string/stream. there is no way to upload a file.
meaning that if we want to upload a file we need to read it, convert it to stream and then upload it.

Please add function `uploadfile(path,content_type)`
which will allow to upload `json`/`csv` files from the local disk.

issue_body:

`

However, when I try to enable it in the Google Cloud Console API Manager (https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/api/datastore.googleapis.com/overview), it shows another error:

This is persistent and happens every time, and I don't have any pending requests to disable any APIs.

The end result?  Absolutely no way to use the Cloud Datastore API!!

issue_title: Checking for default value generates a KeyError if none is set

```
ts = TimeSeries()
hasattr(ts, 'default')
```
it results in a `KeyError`. First, this is a bad behavior (bug) and should be fixed. Secondly, how *should* one properly check if a default is set or not? Maybe a `has_default()` method would be useful for this?

GWF 升级可以识别SS之后，SS何时能升级？魔高一丈道高一丈。
从以前的封IP，到DNS污染，到流量特征识别，防火墙不断进步。

3开发者，个人使用者，企业使用者，各个方面做得利益均衡。促进翻墙技术随时升级和改进。

issue_title: BaseAWSObject has wrong regex for valid_names, specifically for SQS
issue_body:
From AWS:
Please enter a name for your new queue. Queue names must be 1-80 characters in length and be composed of alphanumeric characters, hyphens (-), and underscores (_). Your queue will be created in the US West (Oregon) region.

However, in BaseAWSObject:
valid_names = re.compile(r'^[a-zA-Z0-9]+$')

So using troposphere, I cannot include - or _ in my SQS names even though it's valid.

issue_title: RDS iops can't be set to 0
issue_body:
Allow Iops to be set to 0 for an RDS instance.
While the CF docs say the minimum is 1000, there is a use for setting iops to 0. See http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/CommandLineReference/CLIReference-cmd-RestoreDBInstanceFromDBSnapshot.html for the usage. Basically setting to 0 allows you to restore a provisioned iops snapshot to a non provisioned iops instance.

issue_title: Refactor Task and Subsystem dependencies
support for easy access to compile failures
issue_body:
I have a script that parses the output of a pants compile looking for missing class file errors.  Looking at it now, it's similar to what `suggest-missing-deps` does, but doesn't just look in transitive dependencies but also in a map of package names to jars and at the source tree to find missing internal dependencies.

Right now, this script pipes the output of `./pants compile` to a file and parses that.  It's hard to separate output from one target from output for another, so it needs to try to use some context clues to match compile errors to targets.

I would prefer to have a way of identifying which targets failed in the most recent compile, plus access to compile output from those targets, separated by target (for example on file of output per target).

I can use `compile.zinc.capture_log=True` to have the compiler output in the file debug.log, but from an external script I don't think there is an easy way to find these in the `.pants.d` directories for all the failed targets.

Any suggestions for a good way to get compile output for each failed target from the last run of compile?

issue_title: Configure local or remote process execution via a flag
issue_body:
Right now, we always make a local `CommandRunner` for process execution: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/4e1ea7ed0ea34fbace4c1a543b86ffbb67d099f8/src/rust/engine/src/context.rs#L58-L59

Instead, we should have a pair of flags, perhaps named `remote-execution-server` and `remote-store-server`, which take `host:port` strings, which we pass through to this codepath.
 * If both are set, we should make a `process_execution::remote::CommandRunner`.
 * If the `remote-store-server` is set (whether or not `remote-execution-server` is set), we should make a `Store::with_remote` instead of a `Store::local_only`.
 * If just `remote-execution-server` is set, we should error.
 * If neither are set, we should do what the existing code does.

To pass flags, we need to make new global bootstrap options, add them as new constructor arguments to `Scheduler` in scheduler.py (just like we do for `work_dir` - this is a good template to follow), add them as new arguments to `scheduler_create` in src/rust/engine/lib.rs and the mirror function signature in native.py.

issue_title: Conversion between Targets and their BUILD aliases.
issue_body:
There should be an easy way to do both:

1) Given a Target type, lookup what BUILD aliases map to it.
2) Given a BUILD file alias, lookup what Target type it corresponds to.

The motivation for this is allowing users to specify the names of BUILD file aliases (such as `java_library`) as options, which can be read and understood properly by tasks, subsystems, et cetera, rather than having to use internal names like `JavaLibrary`.

issue_title: Consider using `ClassLoaderCache` in Scala's integration
issue_body:
/cc @stuhood 
When I was having a look at the Scala Zinc integration, I've noticed that you don't use the `ClassLoaderCache` when instantiating Scala's incremental compiler:
```scala
  def newScalaCompiler(instance: XScalaInstance, interfaceJar: File): AnalyzingCompiler =
    new AnalyzingCompiler(
      instance,
      CompilerBridgeProvider.constant(interfaceJar, instance),
      ClasspathOptionsUtil.auto,
      _ => (), None
    )
```
instead of what our default Zinc utils do:  
```scala
  def scalaCompiler(
      scalaInstance: xsbti.compile.ScalaInstance,
      compilerBridgeJar: File,
      classpathOptions: ClasspathOptions
  ): AnalyzingCompiler = {
    val bridgeProvider = constantBridgeProvider(scalaInstance, compilerBridgeJar)
    val emptyHandler = (_: Seq[String]) => ()
    val loader = Some(new ClassLoaderCache(new URLClassLoader(Array())))
    new AnalyzingCompiler(
      scalaInstance,
      bridgeProvider,
      classpathOptions,
      emptyHandler,
      loader
    )
  }
```
  
Note the use of `Some` instead of `None`.
  
Using the classloader cache can provide great speedups, as evidenced by https://github.com/sbt/sbt/pull/2754.
  
Is this an oversight or have you tried this option in the past and didn't happen to improve performance for Pants?

issue_title: junit synthetic classpath does not generate properly encoded MANIFEST.MF
issue_body:
For example, a file path like
```
$ ls org/apache/pig/pig/0.13.0%2B38/pig-0.13.0%2B38-h2.jar
org/apache/pig/pig/0.13.0%2B38/pig-0.13.0%2B38-h2.jar
```
will appear as is in `MANIFEST.MF`

Whereas JVM classloader treats it as a url and tries to decode the path to `org/apache/pig/pig/0.13.0+38/pig-0.13.0+38-h2.jar`, which is not valid anymore, causing ClassNotFound. The proper path to be in MANIFEST.MF is:
```
org/apache/pig/pig/0.13.0%252B38/pig-0.13.0%252B38-h2.jar
```
which gets decoded to `org/apache/pig/pig/0.13.0%2B38/pig-0.13.0%2B38-h2.jar`

issue_title: Adding metadata for source sentences as arguments
issue_body:
Greetings,

Some published NMT architectures rely on adding extra linguistic information to the source sentence. Examples include POS tags (https://www.aclweb.org/anthology/U/U16/U16-1001.pdf) and syntactic trees (http://www.aclweb.org/anthology/P16-1078 https://arxiv.org/pdf/1704.04675.pdf). This issue is an enhancement idea to add provisions to sockeye to read this kind of metadata into the toolkit.

At least initially, the idea is *not* to implement any of these encoders, but just make sure the metadata is properly read and propagated all the way to the encoder interface. I was thinking about having an optional keyword "metadata" to the "encode" method in the Encoder interface. Then users can extend or implement their own encoders while safely assuming the metadata (POS, syntactic trees, etc.) will be available in that metadata field.

I started to implement this idea in the context of adding semantic graphs to the input. Code is being hosted here:
https://github.com/ws17mt/sockeye/tree/source_metadata

I am happy to keep working on this but some feedback would be appreciated. Specifically:
- Is the "metadata keyword" approach the best?
- How does this would affect bucketing? Right now I am just propagating the metadata through the bucketing interface to make sure everything reaches the decoder but the bucketing approaches might not be useful in this setting anymore.
- How to make things work at decoding time?
- I am assuming tokens in the metadata are separated by spaces so I can just reuse the data reading methods. Not sure if this is the best approach though.

issue_title: Inconsistent response formats
issue_body:
The MMS response behavior is currently inconsistent. MMS responds with JSON on a successful API call and responds with HTML when the API call is unsuccessful. For example: `curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1/model/predict -F data=@` returns a JSON with the predictions, but `curl -X POST http://127.0.0.1/model/predict -F data=@` returns a HTML response with the error code like below: ```HTML 400 Bad Request

Bad Request

expected bytes-like object, not str

``` Expected behavior is that it returns JSON on both valid and invalid inputs for consistent client-side handling.

issue_title: backup-fetch does not pull the latest backup when using LATEST as argument (data loss)
issue_body:
**Current behaviour:** 
- When restoring from an Azure Blob Storage (WABS) bucket containing 14 basebackups (and associated WALs), dating from 20th of June to 3rd of July (and not touched since, the last modified date for each of them is the date of their day of creation), using the argument LATEST as the backup name (to get the latest backup). 
- The restore process takes place and completes, but uses the basebackup from the 27th of June. The database then works perfectly but all the data added between the 27th of June and the 2nd of July is lost. 
- When specifying the name of the latest basebackup instead (base_xxxx), it restores perfectly with the latest basebackup and function with all the data. 

**Expected behaviour:**
- When restoring from an Azure Blob Storage (WABS) bucket containing 14 basebackups (and associated WALs), dating from 20th of June to 3rd of July (and not touched since, the last modified date for each of them is the date of their day of creation), using the argument LATEST as the backup name (to get the latest backup). 
- The restore process should take place and complete with the latest basebackup (dating from 3rd of July) without having to specify it by name. Or throw and explicit error if for some reason the latest basebackup is not accessible.

issue_title: Change to make track number have common base
issue_body:
Currently SoCo uses track position that is sometime base 0 and sometimes base 1. This confuses when you retrieve information and then try to use it but need to change it's base. The classic is get_current_track_info() or add_to_queue() which return a base 1 track number, then to use it in play_from queue you need to make it a base  0 track number (subtract 1!). Issue #508 is a perfect example - and started this discussion. 
@ghcs27 notes this is a good idea, but it will be a breaking change however at the moment it is inconsistent.

Below I have documented the scale of the issue as far as I can see it. Please add to this with info you have, and let us have your views. 

SoCo function      | Sonos 	| SoCo           |Notes
------------------- | -----------	| -----------   | ----------------------------------
add_uri_to_queue 	|  base1  |  base1 | although default position is=0!
add_to_queue		|  base1  |  base1 | although default position is=0!
add_multiple_to_queue	| base1  |  base1 |DesiredFirstTrackNumberEnqueued=0
get_current_track_info	| base1  |  base1 |
play_from_queue		| base1  |	base0 |
remove_from_queue	| base1  | base0 |
remove_from_sonos_palylist  | base0 ?  | base0 |
move_in_sonos_laylist |  base0 ?	|  base0 |
reorder_sonos_playlist |   base0 ?	| base0 |

Phonically base 0 would make sense, but it looks like Sonos have largely adopted base 1 !
Thoughts?
Cheers David

issue_title: apply_similar() and create_similar() should import to vsys1 for interfaces with no vsys
issue_body:
In order to mimic the behavior of the firewall GUI which prevents interfaces without any vsys, the `create()` and `apply()` methods check for the `VsysOperations.ALWAYS_IMPORT` constant.  If the constant is True, then the PanObject is imported into vsys1.  All interfaces have the ALWAYS_IMPORT constant set to True.  However, `apply_similar()` and `create_similar()` don't make this check, so the interfaces created by these methods end up without any vsys.

To correct this, `PanObject._requires_import_consideration()` should check for the `ALWAYS_IMPORT` constant, but since this constant exists in `VsysOperations` class, not `PanObject` class, the `_requires_import_consideration()` method should probably be moved to the `VsysOperations` class.

The `PanObject._perform_vsys_dict_import_set()` method should also probably be in the `VsysOperations` class, since it is specific to vsys importable objects.

Hi,

I have several questions about `container.logs`. I'm using Python 3 with docker-py 2.5.1.

* What's the difference between `stream` and `follow`? It looks like if I specify `stream`, it will wait for more logs to come, which is essentially `follow`. On the other hand, if I specify `follow`, then it *has to* be `stream` (how else will I get results?)
* What's the return type. In the docs it says that `string` is returned if not streaming. However, I'm getting `bytes`. I *am* getting `string`s (in the generator) when streaming *at some time* - I haven't been able to identify when it returns `string` vs. `bytes`, but I seem to be getting `string`s in a `ThreadPoolExecutor` environment, and `bytes` if it's in its dedicated process.

Also, I can't get streaming logs for certain containers, consistently, at least if I start the stream as soon as the container is started. There's no error, but it simply hangs forever. Any help is much appreciated. Thanks!

issue_title: Add & Push tags for Releases
TypeError when using None as a key
issue_body:
Executing
```python
from transit.writer import Writer
from io import StringIO
buffer = StringIO()
writer = Writer(buffer)
writer.write({None: 1})
```
results in
```
TypeError: Can't convert 'NoneType' object to str implicitly
```

The issue is in `transit.writer.Marshaler#emit_string` that assumes that its `string` argument is not None.

issue_title: New issues need to be labelled
issue_body: There are many issues that have not been tagged. It would be very useful for newcomers if they find issues with labels.

issue_title: Floating point error in Hill climbing algorithm prevents the procedure from stopping.
issue_body: Once the score gets low enough ( < e-10 ) sometimes the procedure gets stuck in a loop because of very slight change in score by an operation and again a perceived increase by reversing it or doing it again in case of flipping the edge.

issue_title: add --remove-overlap option
issue_body:
We already have a `--keep-overlap` option to skip removing the overlaps while building OTF or TTF.

The overlap removal only happens upon generating binary font formats, not when generating UFOs from glyphs or designspace input.

Sometimes it could be useful to generate instance UFOs with the overlap removal applied. We could do that by adding a `--remove-overlap` option that does that when combined with UFO output `-o ufo`.

issue_title: Support for Django 1.9
issue_body:
The first beta for Django 1.9 has just been released, no more features will come to 1.9, only bugs will be fixed. One could therefore start evaluating what needs to change in cleanerversion to support Django 1.9.

At a first look, I noticed that some pretty things of the ORM implementation pretty essential to cleanerversion have been changed:
- `ValuesQuerySet` and `ValuesListQuerySet` have been removed.
- In `django.db.models.fields.related`, the members `ReverseSingleRelatedObjectDescriptor`, `ReverseManyRelatedObjectsDescriptor`, `ManyRelatedObjectsDescriptor`, `create_many_related_manager`, `ForeignRelatedObjectsDescriptor` have been refactored into new classes at `django.db.models.fields.related_descriptors`.

issue_title: Graceful Upgrade: Restart the routers once
issue_body:
The experimental [graceful upgrade](http://docs.deis.io/en/latest/managing_deis/upgrading-deis/#graceful-upgrade) currently does a rolling restart of routers and an install and start of *all* components which results in restarting the routers twice. 

This results in downtime.

https://github.com/deis/deis/blob/e3a28b48a451338e12165ab769d9c711f9533805/deisctl/cmd/upgrade.go#L101-L104

Output of `deisctl upgrade-takeover`: https://gist.github.com/crigor/22baefac7ac494b02d17

issue_title: Support rolling CoreOS updates
issue_body:
Locking Deis releases to specific CoreOS releases is both inconvenient and not the intended release path for CoreOS - instead, we should be subscribing to the beta channel and using the update-engine service with locksmith to ensure that only one node in a cluster is rebooted at any one time. This is The CoreOS Way(tm).

In order to achieve this in Deis, a few things need to be completed:

* Remove the oneshot unit which disables the CoreOS automatic updater service
* Add locksmith configuration to the user-data file (https://github.com/coreos/coreos-cloudinit/blob/master/Documentation/cloud-config.md#update)
* Bind the deis-database-data and builder-database-data services to the deis-database and deis-builder (respectively) machines, so that fleet will not try to move them when the database or builder machines are rebooted (#1038)

issue_title: AWS provisioning process is fails in 1.9.0+
issue_body:
`gen-json.py` in the AWS provisioning process adds in some AWS-specific user-data and also modifies portions of the user-data for AWS.  In AWS, the CloudFormation templates we use specify launch configuration for CoreOS hosts wherein we mount a few EBS volumes-- among them, we mount one to `/media/etcd`.  One of the corresponding modification to the user-data is to string replace certain instances of `/var/lib/etcd2` with `/media/etcd`

See relevant lines from `gen-json.py` here:

```
# configure etcd to use its EBS volume
dump = dump.replace('-data-dir /var/lib/etcd2', '-data-dir /media/etcd')
dump = dump.replace('--volume=/var/lib/etcd2', '--volume=/media/etcd')
```

https://github.com/deis/deis/blob/master/contrib/aws/gen-json.py#L108-L109

This worked great until v1.9.0 when one of the strings that is ordinarily modified was changed in `user-data.example`.

Here's the relevant commit:

https://github.com/deis/deis/commit/ccb2f9c8ce4dc4d2589bae7ea8f3bc7e87bb315d

What this results in is user-data that's actually used that looks something like this:

```
  - name: etcd.service
    # ...
      [Service]
      # ...
      Environment="ETCD_DATA_DIR=/var/lib/etcd2"
      # ...
      ExecStart=/usr/bin/docker run --net=host --rm \
        --volume=${ETCD_DATA_DIR}:/var/lib/etcd2 \
        # ...
        -data-dir /media/etcd \
        # ...
    # ...
```

As you can see, the wrong directory on the host (should be `/media/etcd`, but is `/var/lib/etcd2`) is mounted into the wrong directory on the container (should be `/media/etcd`, but is `/var/lib/etcd2`).  The containerized etcd2, however, per `-data-dir /media/etcd` is looking for the host's `/media/etcd' to have been mounted into the container's `/media/etcd`... which isn't the case.

This _is_ definitely wrong.  The very odd part about it however, is that whatever issue this actually causes appears to be non-deterministic.  Sometimes this fails spectacularly; sometimes it _somehow_ seems to work (although I'm not sure how).
